title: A flexible digital compute-in-memory chip for edge intelligence

data_scope.arrhythmia_benchmark: MIT-BIH Arrhythmia Database (48 half-hour ECG recordings); 99.2% accuracy on 1-kb chip
data_scope.image_benchmarks: MNIST and Fashion-MNIST (70,000 images each, 28x28); accuracy in Supplementary Table 2
data_scope.activity_monitoring: 4-class daily activity classification; 97.4% accuracy; specific dataset name not stated in main text
data_scope.endurance_data: more than 10^10 fixed and random multiplications without error; >10^4 random input sets
data_scope.bending_cycles: over 40,000 bending cycles at 1 mm radius
data_scope.stability_duration: stable performance for more than 6 months

core_conclusion: FLEXI is a flexible, digital, compute-in-memory AI chip using LTPS thin-film transistor technology that achieves up to 12.5 MHz clock speed and as little as 2.52 mW power at sub-dollar cost, surviving 40,000+ bending cycles. One-shot on-chip neural network deployment reaches 99.2% accuracy in arrhythmia detection and 97.4% accuracy in daily activity monitoring from multimodal physiological signals.
why_it_matters: First fully digital, reconfigurable compute-in-memory architecture on a flexible substrate capable of real neural-network inference; sub-dollar cost at commercial yield removes the fabrication barrier; one-shot deployment eliminates write latency; achieves clinically relevant benchmark accuracy; sets a new durability bar with 40,000+ bending cycles.
why_nature: Platform-level hardware breakthrough with broad impact on robotics, wearables, and HMI; process-circuit-algorithm co-design is a paradigm advance; quantified performance across multiple inference domains; open data (Zenodo) and code (GitHub) support reproducibility.
added_value_to_my_research: Bayesian active learning on CiM hardware -> on-device edge-deployable GeoAI with selective spatial label querying to reduce annotation cost in health geography
Random sketch compression of spatiotemporal GNN weights -> privacy-preserving on-device inference for sensitive health and location data in low-resource settings

In-memory reservoir computing for multimodal sensor fusion -> sensor-side fusion of heterogeneous data streams (mobile phone, remote sensing, POIs) preserving privacy in field health-geography surveys
SNN dynamics on FLEXI's RLPU -> continuous ultra-low-power ambient health monitoring for spatial health equity research in underserved communities
Functionally invariant path traversal via reconfigurable CiM -> multi-city, multi-task geospatial foundation models that share weights but adapt to local spatial context

related.title: Deep Bayesian active learning using in-memory computing hardware
related.relation: active learning loop on in-memory hardware extends FLEXI's static deployment to continual adaptation

related.title: Random sketch learning for deep neural networks in edge computing
related.relation: random sketch compression enables larger effective models on FLEXI's 1-kb SRAM capacity

related.title: Energy-efficient multimodal zero-shot learning using in-memory reservoir computing
related.relation: reservoir computing layer for multimodal sensor fusion compatible with FLEXI's reconfigurable RLPU

related.title: Deep learning incorporating biologically inspired neural dynamics and in-memory computing
related.relation: SNN dynamics on in-memory hardware can be mapped onto FLEXI's digital RLPU for ultra-low-power temporal coding

related.title: Engineering flexible machine learning systems by traversing functionally invariant paths
related.relation: functionally invariant paths enable structured multi-task weight masking on FLEXI's reconfigurable architecture

figures.caption: Overview of FLEXI and its key attributes — the flexible chip concept, fabrication stack, and summary of performance specifications
Circuit architecture and algorithm implementation — SRAM-based CiM with RLPU, memory/compute mode switching, and quantized NN deployment
Performance characteristics — clock frequency, power, yield, endurance over 10^10 multiplications, and mechanical bending stability
FLEXI for daily monitoring and activity classification — physiological signal pipeline, weight distribution on chip, and confusion matrix for 4-class activity recognition
